header: S. S. da Grama (SP) / Pocos de Caldas (MG)

lesson.menu_desc: Banks, Cash & Cards -- Second conditional (if + past, would) -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 13 -- Money Matters -- Banks, Cash & Cards
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Tuca Dias | Aula 13 -- Money Matters

listenings.text: Good afternoon, and welcome to City Central Bank. Please listen to this short message before you go to the counter. If you need cash, you can use one of our cash machines in the hall. They are open all day and all night. The daily limit at the machine is five hundred dollars. If you want to take out more than that, you have to come to a counter and show your passport. To change money, please go to the exchange desk on your right. Today's exchange rate is shown on the screen above the desk. Remember, there is a small fee for every exchange, so please check the rate and the fee before you accept. If you lose your card, you must call the number on the back of your bank statement at once. We will block the card to keep your account safe. Keep your receipt for every transaction. If you have any questions, our staff will be happy to help. Thank you for banking with us.

Hi Tuca, it is your friend again. I am calling about money for our trip next month. Here is my advice. First, do not carry too much cash. If I were you, I would take a little cash for the first day and use my card for everything else. Before you travel, call your bank and tell them the dates. If you do not warn them, they might block your card when you pay abroad. At the airport, do not change money at the first desk you see. The exchange rate there is usually bad and the fee is high. If I had more time, I would change a small amount at home and the rest at a city bank. Also, always keep your receipt and check your balance online every few days. If you saw a payment you did not make, you would call the bank straight away. One last thing: keep one card in your bag and a second one in the hotel safe. If you lost one, you would still have the other. Have a great trip, and talk soon. Bye.

extra_audio.text: First, Tuca went to the bank to change some money for her trip. Then she checked the exchange rate and the fee on the screen. After that, she asked the teller to withdraw two hundred dollars in cash. Next, she took her cash and kept the receipt in her bag. Finally, she checked her account balance online to make sure everything was correct.
